card.intuition: A standard awareness document for developers and web application security, representing a broad consensus on the most critical security risks to web applications.
card.analogy: The 'Top 10 Most Wanted' list for hackers: it tells you exactly what they are looking for so you can lock the right doors and windows first.
when_to_use: Designing the security architecture of a new application
Training team members on common vulnerabilities
Prioritizing which bugs to fix during a security audit
gotchas: The list changes every few years! What was #1 in 2017 might be #3 in 2021 as new technologies (like microservices and cloud) change the attack landscape
Fixing all 10 doesn't make you 'uncrackable', but it does protect you against ~90% of common automated attacks

exercise.prompt: Identify the #1 risk in the most recent OWASP Top 10 (2021).
exercise.starter_code:
top_risk = "_______ Control (A01:2021)"
# It means a user can access data they shouldn't see.
hints: If the door isn't locked correctly, the access is 'Broken'
